---
title: "Results"
subtitle: "Complete three-country comparison"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/metrics.json").json()
subperiodData = FileAttachment("_data/subperiod_metrics.json").json()
gapData = FileAttachment("_data/gap_data.json").json()
ablationData = FileAttachment("_data/ablation_data.json").json()
searchComparison = FileAttachment("_data/search_comparison.json").json()
pipelineConfigs = FileAttachment("_data/pipeline_configs.json").json()
Plot = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6/+esm")
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedCountry = Inputs.radio(
  ["norway", "canada", "sweden"],
  {label: "Country", value: "norway",
   format: v => ({"norway": "Norway", "canada": "Canada", "sweden": "Sweden"})[v]}
)

countryTargets = ({
  "norway": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "canada": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "sweden": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "unemployment"]
})
```

---

## Best pipeline discovered

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const cfg = pipelineConfigs[selectedCountry];
  if (!cfg) return html`<p>No config available.</p>`;
  const bc = cfg.best_config;
  const covs = bc.covariates?.join(", ") || "none";
  const tx = bc.transforms && Object.keys(bc.transforms).length > 0
    ? Object.entries(bc.transforms).map(([k,v]) => `${k}: ${v}`).join(", ")
    : "none";
  const ctx = bc.context_length || "unlimited";
  const ft = bc.fine_tune ? `Yes (${bc.fine_tune_steps} steps, lr=${bc.fine_tune_lr})` : "No";

  return html`
    <table class="table table-sm">
      <tr><td><strong>Covariates</strong></td><td>${covs}</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Transforms</strong></td><td>${tx}</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Context length</strong></td><td>${ctx}</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Fine-tuning</strong></td><td>${ft}</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Val. MASE</strong></td><td>${cfg.best_score.toFixed(3)}</td></tr>
    </table>
  `;
}
```

---

## Validation era (2006--2015)

All results below are from the **rolling pseudo-real-time validation** with 120 monthly forecast origins. MASE is shown (< 1 beats random walk).

### Summary table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const valMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation"
  );

  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "var", "factor", "zero_shot"];
  const methodSet = new Set(valMetrics.map(d => d.method));
  const methods = methodOrder.filter(m => methodSet.has(m));

  const rows = methods.map(method => {
    const mData = valMetrics.filter(d => d.method === method);
    const row = {Method: mData[0]?.display_name || method};
    for (const h of [1, 3, 6, 12]) {
      const hVals = mData.filter(d => d.horizon === h).map(d => d.mase).filter(v => v != null);
      const avg = hVals.length > 0 ? hVals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / hVals.length : null;
      row[`h=${h}`] = avg != null ? avg.toFixed(3) : "---";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "h=1", "h=3", "h=6", "h=12"],
    width: {Method: 200}
  });
}
```

### Heatmap

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-heatmap
//| fig-cap: "Validation MASE heatmap --- all methods, variables, and horizons. Darker green = lower MASE (better)."

{
  const valMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "validation"
  );

  const vars = countryTargets[selectedCountry];
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];
  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "var", "factor", "zero_shot"];

  const data = valMetrics
    .filter(d => methodOrder.includes(d.method) && vars.includes(d.target) && d.mase != null)
    .map(d => ({
      method: d.display_name,
      cell: `${d.target.replace(/_/g, " ")} h=${d.horizon}`,
      value: d.mase
    }));

  const methodNames = methodOrder
    .map(m => valMetrics.find(d => d.method === m)?.display_name)
    .filter(Boolean);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 300,
    marginLeft: 180,
    marginBottom: 50,
    padding: 0.05,
    x: {
      label: null,
      domain: vars.flatMap(v => horizons.map(h => `${v.replace(/_/g, " ")} h=${h}`)),
      tickRotate: -45,
      tickSize: 0
    },
    y: {
      label: null,
      domain: methodNames.slice().reverse()
    },
    color: {
      scheme: "greens",
      reverse: true,
      label: "MASE",
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.cell(data, {
        x: "cell",
        y: "method",
        fill: "value",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.method}\n${d.cell}\nMASE: ${d.value.toFixed(4)}`
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

---

## Test era (2016--2025)

Out-of-sample evaluation on **111 monthly origins** (2016-01 to 2025-03). The agent-tuned config was locked from the search --- no further optimization.

### Full test-era comparison

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const testMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "test"
  );

  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "var", "factor", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];
  const methodSet = new Set(testMetrics.map(d => d.method));
  const methods = methodOrder.filter(m => methodSet.has(m));

  const rows = methods.map(method => {
    const mData = testMetrics.filter(d => d.method === method);
    const row = {Method: mData[0]?.display_name || method};
    for (const h of [1, 3, 6, 12]) {
      const hVals = mData.filter(d => d.horizon === h).map(d => d.mase).filter(v => v != null);
      const avg = hVals.length > 0 ? hVals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / hVals.length : null;
      row[`h=${h}`] = avg != null ? avg.toFixed(3) : "---";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "h=1", "h=3", "h=6", "h=12"],
    width: {Method: 220}
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-test-lines
//| fig-cap: "Test-era MASE by horizon for key methods. MASE < 1 (dashed) beats the random walk."

{
  const testMetrics = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === selectedCountry && d.era === "test"
  );

  const showMethods = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];

  const grouped = {};
  for (const d of testMetrics) {
    if (!showMethods.includes(d.method)) continue;
    const key = `${d.method}_${d.horizon}`;
    if (!grouped[key]) grouped[key] = {method: d.display_name, horizon: d.horizon, category: d.category, vals: []};
    if (d.mase != null) grouped[key].vals.push(d.mase);
  }

  const data = Object.values(grouped)
    .filter(g => g.vals.length > 0)
    .map(g => ({...g, mase: g.vals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / g.vals.length}))
    .filter(d => d.mase < 3);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 400,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Forecast horizon (months)", ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]},
    y: {label: "Average MASE", grid: true},
    color: {
      domain: ["Random Walk", "ARIMA", "BVAR", "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)", "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)"],
      range: ["#95a5a6", "#2ecc71", "#3498db", "#9b59b6", "#e74c3c"]
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4"}),
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", fill: "method",
        r: 5, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```


### Subperiod breakdown

The test era spans three distinct macroeconomic regimes.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedSubperiod = Inputs.select(
  ["pre_covid", "covid", "post_covid"],
  {label: "Subperiod", value: "pre_covid",
   format: v => ({"pre_covid": "Pre-COVID (2016-2019)", "covid": "COVID (2020-2021)", "post_covid": "Post-COVID / Inflation (2022+)"})[v]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-subperiod
//| fig-cap: "Average RMSE across target variables by method and horizon for the selected subperiod."

{
  const showMethods = ["random_walk", "arima", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];
  const methodNames = {
    random_walk: "Random Walk", arima: "ARIMA",
    zero_shot: "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)", agent_tuned: "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)"
  };
  const targets = countryTargets[selectedCountry];

  const data = showMethods.flatMap(method => {
    return [1, 3, 6, 12].map(h => {
      const vals = subperiodData
        .filter(r => r.country === selectedCountry && r.subperiod === selectedSubperiod
          && r.method === method && r.horizon === h && targets.includes(r.target))
        .map(r => r.rmse)
        .filter(v => v != null);
      return {
        method: methodNames[method],
        horizon: h,
        value: vals.length > 0 ? vals.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / vals.length : null
      };
    });
  }).filter(d => d.value != null);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Forecast horizon (months)", ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]},
    y: {label: "Average RMSE", grid: true},
    color: {
      domain: Object.values(methodNames),
      range: ["#95a5a6", "#2ecc71", "#9b59b6", "#e74c3c"]
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", fill: "method",
        r: 5, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

---

## Validation-to-test gap

The central question: do validation-era gains survive out of sample?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-gap
//| fig-cap: "MASE on validation vs test era, by country and config. Bars above the dashed line (MASE=1) are worse than the random walk."

{
  const data = gapData.map(d => ({
    country: d.country.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + d.country.slice(1),
    config: d.config,
    era: d.era === "validation" ? "Validation" : "Test",
    mase: d.avg_mase
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {label: null, padding: 0.2},
    y: {label: "Average MASE", grid: true, domain: [0.7, 1.2]},
    fx: {label: null},
    color: {
      domain: ["Validation", "Test"],
      range: ["#3498db", "#e74c3c"],
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4", strokeWidth: 1}),
      Plot.barY(data, Plot.groupX({y: "identity"}, {
        x: "config", y: "mase", fill: "era", fx: "country",
        tip: true
      }))
    ]
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const rows = ["norway", "canada", "sweden"].map(c => {
    const zsVal = gapData.find(d => d.country === c && d.config === "Zero-shot" && d.era === "validation");
    const zsTest = gapData.find(d => d.country === c && d.config === "Zero-shot" && d.era === "test");
    const atVal = gapData.find(d => d.country === c && d.config === "Agent-tuned" && d.era === "validation");
    const atTest = gapData.find(d => d.country === c && d.config === "Agent-tuned" && d.era === "test");

    const atGap = atVal && atTest && atVal.avg_mase > 0
      ? ((atTest.avg_mase - atVal.avg_mase) / atVal.avg_mase * 100)
      : null;

    return {
      Country: c.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + c.slice(1),
      "ZS Val": zsVal?.avg_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---",
      "ZS Test": zsTest?.avg_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---",
      "AT Val": atVal?.avg_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---",
      "AT Test": atTest?.avg_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---",
      "Gap (%)": atGap != null ? `${atGap > 0 ? "+" : ""}${atGap.toFixed(1)}%` : "---",
      "AT h=12 Test": atTest?.h12_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---"
    };
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Country", "ZS Val", "ZS Test", "AT Val", "AT Test", "Gap (%)", "AT h=12 Test"],
    width: {Country: 80}
  });
}
```

**Key pattern:** Norway's agent-tuned config (which relies on fine-tuning) degrades +11% on the test era. Sweden's config (covariate selection without fine-tuning) actually improves by 1.2%.

---

## Ablation: what components matter?

Leave-one-out analysis: remove each component from the best config and measure the degradation in validation MASE.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-ablation
//| fig-cap: "Leave-one-out ablation. Bars show % change in MASE when each component is removed. Positive = worse."

{
  const abl = ablationData[selectedCountry];
  if (!abl || !abl.ablations) return html`<p>No ablation data available.</p>`;

  const data = abl.ablations.map(a => ({
    component: a.name.replace("drop_", "").replace(/_/g, " "),
    degradation: a.degradation_pct || 0,
    score: a.score
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: Math.max(250, data.length * 35 + 80),
    marginLeft: 140,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Change in MASE (%)", grid: true},
    y: {label: null, domain: data.map(d => d.component)},
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#999"}),
      Plot.barX(data, {
        x: "degradation", y: "component",
        fill: d => d.degradation > 0 ? "#e74c3c" : "#2ecc71",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.component}\nMASE: ${d.score?.toFixed(3) || '---'}\nDeg: ${d.degradation?.toFixed(2)}%`
      }),
      Plot.text(data, {
        x: "degradation", y: "component",
        text: d => `${d.degradation > 0 ? "+" : ""}${d.degradation?.toFixed(2)}%`,
        dx: d => d.degradation >= 0 ? 5 : -5,
        textAnchor: d => d.degradation >= 0 ? "start" : "end",
        fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

Reference (full config) MASE: **`{ojs} ablationData[selectedCountry]?.reference_score?.toFixed(3) || "---"`**

---

## Search method comparison

How do different search strategies compare on validation MASE?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-search-comparison
//| fig-cap: "Validation MASE by search method and country. Lower is better."

{
  const methods = ["Informed LLM", "Blind LLM", "Random", "Greedy", "Manual economist", "Zero-shot baseline"];
  const data = searchComparison
    .filter(d => d.val_mase != null)
    .map(d => ({
      method: d.method,
      country: d.display_country,
      mase: d.val_mase
    }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 120,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {label: "Validation MASE", grid: true},
    y: {label: null, domain: methods},
    fx: {label: null},
    color: {
      domain: ["Norway", "Canada", "Sweden"],
      range: ["#3498db", "#e74c3c", "#f39c12"],
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "mase", y: "method", fill: "country",
        r: 6, tip: true
      }),
      // Connect with thin lines per country
      Plot.line(data.filter(d => d.country === "Norway"), {
        x: "mase", y: "method", stroke: "#3498db", strokeWidth: 0.5, strokeOpacity: 0.3
      }),
      Plot.line(data.filter(d => d.country === "Canada"), {
        x: "mase", y: "method", stroke: "#e74c3c", strokeWidth: 0.5, strokeOpacity: 0.3
      }),
      Plot.line(data.filter(d => d.country === "Sweden"), {
        x: "mase", y: "method", stroke: "#f39c12", strokeWidth: 0.5, strokeOpacity: 0.3
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const methods = ["Informed LLM", "Blind LLM", "Random", "Greedy", "Manual economist", "Zero-shot baseline"];
  const rows = methods.map(m => {
    const row = {Method: m};
    for (const c of ["norway", "canada", "sweden"]) {
      const d = searchComparison.find(r => r.method === m && r.country === c);
      row[c.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + c.slice(1)] = d?.val_mase?.toFixed(3) || "---";
    }
    return row;
  });
  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "Norway", "Canada", "Sweden"],
    width: {Method: 160}
  });
}
```
